name: Pan-Broiled Steak with Tomatoes & Garlic-Ital
recipeIngredient: 3 pounds beef T-bone, porterhouse or sirloin steak (1 inch thick)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a heavy 8-inch skillet over moderate heat.
2. Remove the pan from the heat. Add the garlic and stir for 30 seconds with a wooden spoon.
3. Add the tomatoes, oregano, half teaspoon of salt, and a few grindings of pepper. Cook over moderate heat, stirring with the wooden spoon.
4. When the tomato liquid has boiled away, remove the pan from the heat.
5. In a 10-inch skillet, heat the remaining two tablespoons of olive oil. Over high heat, brown the steak in the oil for a couple of minutes on each side.
6. Reduce the heat to moderate and spoon the tomato sauce over and around the meat. Cover and cook for three to five minutes, or until the steak is done to your taste (test by making a small incision near the bone and judging the redness of the meat).
7. Scrape off the tomato sauce and transfer the steak to a carving board.
8. Simmer the sauce in the pan for 1-2 minutes, scraping in any browned bits from the bottom. Taste for seasoning.
9. Slice the steak and serve with the sauce.
